feedback: The proof slice stays within the frontend working root and does not require protected-path access.
The empty-state change is aligned with the meal-planner route structure and directs users toward an existing meal-plan flow.
Repo-native validation can be attached using the commands defined by the repo profile.
evidence: Target repo profile identifies frontend as a safe working root and .github/.claude/.copilot/.squad/meal_planner.db/target as protected paths.
Canonical validation commands are available: cargo test; cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy && cd frontend && npm run lint; cargo build --release && cd frontend && npm run build.
The planned change references real meal-planner files: frontend/src/routes/shopping-list/index.tsx and frontend/src/routes/meal-plans/index.tsx.
